channel.title: Blog Posts on Mirai Data
channel.description: Recent content in Blog Posts on Mirai Data

item.title: 'Mataró out loud' wins the Catalan Dataviz Award from the ViT Foundation
item.description: The project Mataró out loud , the scrollstorytelling about noise levels in the city of Mataró, published by Mirai Data, has been recognised with the Català DataViz award and has also been a finalist in the Young Talent category of the Women DataViz 2025 Awards, organised by the ViT Foundation .

item.title: MiraiData provides data training at the European Commission's JRC
item.description: Last week, MiraiData travelled to Seville to deliver a two-day training course on data analysis, visualisation and communication at the European Commission’s JRC.

item.title: Mataró out loud
item.description: Traffic, terraces, markets, construction sites, public festivals... Everyday life in cities is noisy.

item.title: I only know how much I get paid, the rest...
item.description: Definitions matter as much as the outcomes themselves. Because… What happens when those outcomes directly affect people’s wallets?

item.title: #30DayMapChallenge 2024
item.description: Similarly to the #30DayChartChallenge in which we participated in April , during November the map community celebrates the #30DayMapChallenge and we have joined in!

item.title: Is government data always reliable?
item.description: In this post I am going to tell you a short anecdote that happened to me recently.

item.title: #30DayChartChallenge 2024
item.description: This year we actively participated in the #30DayChartChallenge and here are the results!

item.title: II Congress & XIII Conference of R Users in Barcelona
item.description: This year we have returned to the II Congress and XIII Conference of the R Hispano Community, which were held in Barcelona and hosted by Pompeu Fabra University.

item.title: I Congress & XII Conference of R Users in Córdoba
item.description: On 23, 24 and 25 November, we had the opportunity to attend the annual meeting organised by the R Hispano Community, which this year was hosted by the University of Córdoba.
